Reviews Contact
Home / Services / Vinyl Fencing

Vinyl Fencing

Low-maintenance alternative that resists fading in intense Texas sun.

Premium PVC fencing systems engineered for San Antonio's UV index, using formulations with titanium dioxide that prevent yellowing. Unlike cheaper vinyl that warps in our heat, we use 7" thick boards with aluminum reinforcement channels - crucial for maintaining straight lines during 100° summers.

Available in classic white or sandstone tones that complement local limestone, with texture options from smooth contemporary to wood-grain patterns. All posts include our patented ventilation system that prevents moisture buildup behind panels, a common failure point in humid microclimates near the River Walk.

Perfect for pool enclosures where splashed chlorinated water would damage wood. We use salt-resistant hardware and make all cuts onsite with industrial saws that prevent the chipping common with pre-cut materials. Gates feature stainless steel internal frames for wobble-free operation year after year.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Thermal imaging to identify heat zones affecting expansion
  2. 02 Step 2: Color matching to your home's exterior
  3. 03 Step 3: Custom fabrication avoiding weak seams
  4. 04 Step 4: Installation with expansion joints for temperature swings

Benefits

  • Never needs painting or staining
  • Won't harbor termites common in SA
  • Easy cleaning with garden hose
  • 50-year manufacturer warranty

Frequently Asked Questions

How does vinyl handle hail storms?

Our 5/8" thick panels are rated for 1" hail at 50mph. For added protection, we can install with removable foam inserts during peak storm seasons.

Can you match my existing vinyl fence?

We maintain samples from discontinued lines and can custom blend colors to approximate older sections needing repair.

Is it slippery when wet?

Our textured surfaces maintain traction even during heavy rains, unlike cheaper smooth vinyl that becomes hazardous on slopes common in Terrell Hills.

Get a Free Estimate

Ready to get started with vinyl fencing? Contact us today.
